command_or_instruction: Download and install from https://nodejs.org/ (LTS version recommended)
expected_result: Run 'node --version' and see v18.x or higher
notes: Required for CDK and frontend build tools

command_or_instruction: Download and install from https://python.org/ (check 'Add to PATH' during install)
expected_result: Run 'python3 --version' and see 3.12.x or higher
notes: Required for Lambda function code and dependency installation

command_or_instruction: Download and install from https://aws.amazon.com/cli/
expected_result: Run 'aws --version' and see aws-cli/2.x
notes: Required for all AWS operations

command_or_instruction: Run: aws configure -- Enter your AWS Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, default region 'us-east-1', and output format 'json'
expected_result: Run 'aws sts get-caller-identity' and see your account ID
notes: Your IAM user needs Administrator access for CDK deployment

command_or_instruction: Run: npm install -g aws-cdk
expected_result: Run 'cdk --version' and see 2.x
notes: CDK CLI is used to deploy infrastructure

command_or_instruction: Run: git --version
expected_result: See git version 2.x or higher
notes: Needed if you clone the repo; skip if you already have the files

command_or_instruction: Open https://console.aws.amazon.com/ and sign in. Ensure region is set to us-east-1 (N. Virginia) in the top-right dropdown
expected_result: You are logged into the AWS Console in us-east-1
notes: All resources must be in us-east-1 for Bedrock model availability

command_or_instruction: In the AWS Console search bar type 'Bedrock' and click Amazon Bedrock. Then click 'Model access' in the left sidebar
expected_result: You see the Model access page listing available models
notes: This page controls which AI models your account can use

command_or_instruction: Click 'Manage model access', find 'Amazon > Titan Text Embeddings V2', check the box, click 'Request model access'
expected_result: Status shows 'Access granted' for Titan Text Embeddings V2
notes: This model converts text into vectors for search. Usually instant approval

command_or_instruction: Find 'Anthropic > Claude 3.5 Haiku' in the model list, check the box, click 'Request model access'
expected_result: Status shows 'Access granted' for Claude 3.5 Haiku
notes: This model generates draft documents. May take a few minutes for approval

command_or_instruction: Find 'Anthropic > Claude Sonnet 4' in the model list, check the box, click 'Request model access'
expected_result: Status shows 'Access granted' for Claude Sonnet 4
notes: This model performs document audits. May take a few minutes for approval

command_or_instruction: Refresh the Model access page until all three models show 'Access granted'
expected_result: All three models show 'Access granted'
notes: Do not proceed until all models are approved

command_or_instruction: Open https://whatismyip.com in your browser and note your IPv4 address
expected_result: You have your public IP address (e.g., 203.0.113.45)
notes: This IP will be added to the WAF allowlist

command_or_instruction: Open cdk/lib/config.ts in a text editor. Find the ALLOWED_IPS array. Replace '0.0.0.0/32' with 'YOUR.IP.ADDRESS/32' (keep the /32 suffix)
expected_result: The ALLOWED_IPS array contains your actual IP with /32 suffix
notes: Add multiple IPs by adding more entries to the array. The /32 means single IP

command_or_instruction: In cdk/lib/config.ts review CATEGORIES, BEDROCK_MODELS, and RESOURCE_NAMES. Adjust if needed.
expected_result: Config file has correct settings for your use case
notes: Default settings work for most users. Categories can be modified later

command_or_instruction: Open a terminal and run: cd path/to/AWSClaude/cdk
expected_result: You are in the AWSClaude/cdk directory
notes: All CDK commands must be run from this directory

command_or_instruction: Run: npm install
expected_result: node_modules directory is created with no errors
notes: This installs TypeScript, CDK libraries, and other dependencies

command_or_instruction: Run: cd ../backend
expected_result: You are in the AWSClaude/backend directory
notes: Lambda function code and dependencies are here

command_or_instruction: Run: mkdir -p layers/dependencies/python
expected_result: Directory layers/dependencies/python/ is created
notes: This is where Python packages will be installed for the Lambda layer

command_or_instruction: Run: pip install -r requirements.txt -t layers/dependencies/python/
expected_result: Python packages (pypdf, python-docx, boto3) are installed in layers/dependencies/python/
notes: This bundles the dependencies so Lambda can use them. May show warnings about pip version - these are safe to ignore

command_or_instruction: Run: cd ../cdk
expected_result: You are in the AWSClaude/cdk directory
notes: CDK commands must run from this directory

command_or_instruction: Run: cdk bootstrap
expected_result: Output shows 'CDKToolkit' stack created or already exists
notes: Bootstrap creates an S3 bucket and IAM roles that CDK needs. Only needed once per account/region

command_or_instruction: Run: cdk deploy --all --require-approval broadening
expected_result: Two stacks deploy: RagToolStack and RagToolBedrockStack. You may be prompted to approve IAM changes - type 'y' and press Enter
notes: This creates all AWS resources: S3 buckets, Lambda functions, API Gateway, CloudFront, WAF, and Bedrock KB. Takes 5-10 minutes

command_or_instruction: After deployment completes, note the output values: DistributionUrl, FrontendBucketName, DocumentsBucketName, ApiUrl, KnowledgeBaseId, DataSourceId
expected_result: You have recorded all output values
notes: These values are needed for subsequent steps. You can also retrieve them later with: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name RagToolStack

command_or_instruction: In AWS Console go to Amazon Bedrock > Knowledge bases. Find 'rag-tool-kb'
expected_result: Knowledge base 'rag-tool-kb' exists in the console
notes: If it doesn't appear, check the CloudFormation stack events for errors

command_or_instruction: Click on 'rag-tool-kb' then check the Data sources tab. Verify it points to your documents S3 bucket
expected_result: Data source shows your documents bucket ARN
notes: If the data source is misconfigured, you can edit it in the console

command_or_instruction: If the vector store shows as 'PLACEHOLDER' or needs configuration: In the KB console, edit the vector store configuration to use OpenSearch Serverless or S3 Vectors
expected_result: Vector store is properly configured and shows 'Active'
notes: S3 Vectors is cheaper but may require manual setup. OpenSearch Serverless is more automated but has a $700/month minimum

command_or_instruction: Run: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name RagToolBedrockStack --query "Stacks[0].Outputs[?OutputKey=='KnowledgeBaseId'].OutputValue" --output text
expected_result: A string like 'XXXXXXXXXX' is printed
notes: Save this value - it's needed for Lambda configuration

command_or_instruction: Run: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name RagToolBedrockStack --query "Stacks[0].Outputs[?OutputKey=='DataSourceId'].OutputValue" --output text
expected_result: A string like 'XXXXXXXXXX' is printed
notes: Save this value - it's needed for Lambda configuration and KB sync

command_or_instruction: Run: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name RagToolStack --query "Stacks[0].Outputs[?OutputKey=='DocumentsBucketName'].OutputValue" --output text
expected_result: Bucket name like '123456789012-rag-documents' is printed
notes: Save this value

command_or_instruction: For each Lambda function name starting with 'RagToolStack-': Run 'aws lambda update-function-configuration --function-name FUNCTION_NAME --environment Variables={KNOWLEDGE_BASE_ID=KB_ID_HERE,DATA_SOURCE_ID=DS_ID_HERE,DOCUMENTS_BUCKET=BUCKET_NAME_HERE}' replacing the placeholder values with those from steps 28-30
expected_result: Each Lambda shows 'LastUpdateStatus: Successful'
notes: You can find exact function names with: aws lambda list-functions --query "Functions[?starts_with(FunctionName,'RagToolStack')].FunctionName" --output text

command_or_instruction: Run: cd ../frontend (from the cdk directory)
expected_result: You are in the AWSClaude/frontend directory
notes: Frontend source code is here

command_or_instruction: Run: npm install
expected_result: node_modules directory created with no errors
notes: Installs React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and other frontend dependencies

command_or_instruction: Run: npm run build
expected_result: A 'dist' directory is created containing index.html and JS/CSS bundles
notes: This compiles TypeScript and bundles the React app for production

command_or_instruction: Run: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name RagToolStack --query "Stacks[0].Outputs[?OutputKey=='FrontendBucketName'].OutputValue" --output text
expected_result: Bucket name like '123456789012-rag-frontend' is printed
notes: This is where the built frontend files will be uploaded

command_or_instruction: Run: aws s3 sync dist/ s3://FRONTEND_BUCKET_NAME/ --delete (replace FRONTEND_BUCKET_NAME with the value from step 35)
expected_result: Output shows files uploaded to S3
notes: The --delete flag removes old files that are no longer in the build

command_or_instruction: Run: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name RagToolStack --query "Stacks[0].Outputs[?OutputKey=='DistributionId'].OutputValue" --output text
expected_result: Distribution ID like 'E1234567890ABC' is printed
notes: Needed for cache invalidation

command_or_instruction: Run: aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id DISTRIBUTION_ID --paths '/*' (replace DISTRIBUTION_ID with value from step 37)
expected_result: Output shows invalidation created with status 'InProgress'
notes: This ensures users see the latest version of the frontend. Takes 1-2 minutes to complete

command_or_instruction: Run: aws s3api put-object --bucket DOCUMENTS_BUCKET --key 'tech-approach/' --content-length 0 (repeat for organizational-approach/ past-performance/ and resumes/)
expected_result: Each command returns an ETag confirming the object was created
notes: These empty objects act as folder markers in S3

command_or_instruction: Run: aws s3 cp your-file.pdf s3://DOCUMENTS_BUCKET/tech-approach/ (adjust the category folder and filename)
expected_result: File appears in S3 under the correct category folder
notes: Repeat for each document you want to add. Use the correct category prefix

command_or_instruction: Run: aws bedrock-agent start-ingestion-job --knowledge-base-id KB_ID --data-source-id DS_ID (replace with your actual IDs)
expected_result: Output shows ingestionJob with status 'STARTING'
notes: This processes your S3 documents into searchable embeddings

command_or_instruction: Run: aws bedrock-agent get-ingestion-job --knowledge-base-id KB_ID --data-source-id DS_ID --ingestion-job-id JOB_ID (use the job ID from step 41 output)
expected_result: Status changes from 'IN_PROGRESS' to 'COMPLETE'
notes: Check every 30 seconds. Large document sets may take several minutes

command_or_instruction: Run: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name RagToolStack --query "Stacks[0].Outputs[?OutputKey=='DistributionUrl'].OutputValue" --output text
expected_result: URL like 'https://d1234567890.cloudfront.net' is printed
notes: This is the URL you'll use to access the application

command_or_instruction: Open the CloudFront URL in your browser from a machine with an allowed IP
expected_result: You see the RAG Document Tool web interface with Search & Generate, File Manager, and Audit tabs
notes: If you get a 403 error, your IP is not in the allowlist. Update config.ts and redeploy

command_or_instruction: Click 'File Manager' tab. Select a file and category, then click 'Upload File'
expected_result: File appears in the file list and a success message is shown
notes: Verify the file also appears in S3 using: aws s3 ls s3://DOCUMENTS_BUCKET/category-prefix/

command_or_instruction: Click 'Search & Generate' tab. Enter a query, select a category, and click search
expected_result: Search results appear showing matching documents with passages and scores
notes: If no results appear, ensure KB sync completed successfully (step 42)

command_or_instruction: Select one or more search results and click 'Proceed with Selected'. Wait for generation
expected_result: A draft document is generated matching the tone and style of selected references
notes: Generation may take 10-30 seconds depending on the amount of text

command_or_instruction: Click 'Audit' tab. Upload a document and a criteria file, then click 'Run Audit'
expected_result: A structured audit report appears with criteria, verdicts, evidence, and recommendations
notes: Audit may take 1-2 minutes for long documents

command_or_instruction: Upload via the File Manager UI or CLI: aws s3 cp file.pdf s3://BUCKET/category/
expected_result: File is uploaded to S3
notes: Remember to run KB sync after adding documents via CLI

command_or_instruction: Run: ./scripts/sync-knowledge-base.sh (or run the aws bedrock-agent start-ingestion-job command manually)
expected_result: Ingestion job completes and new documents are searchable
notes: UI uploads automatically trigger sync; CLI uploads require manual sync

command_or_instruction: 1. Edit cdk/lib/config.ts and add entry to CATEGORIES array. 2. Run 'cd cdk && cdk deploy --all'. 3. Create S3 folder. 4. Upload docs. 5. Sync KB
expected_result: New category appears in all UI dropdowns
notes: See README.md 'Adding New Categories' section for details

command_or_instruction: 1. Edit cdk/lib/config.ts ALLOWED_IPS array. 2. Run 'cd cdk && cdk deploy --all'
expected_result: New IPs can access the tool; removed IPs are blocked
notes: Changes take effect within minutes after deployment
